Title: Pre-visit camera, microphone and speaker self-test passes on supported browser
Preconditions: Patient invited to a scheduled video visit; supported browser with granted media permissions
Test_Steps: 1. Open the visit join link before the scheduled time | 2. Launch the device self-test wizard | 3. Allow camera, microphone and speaker access | 4. Observe local video preview, mic input meter and play test tone
Expected_Result: Self-test reports camera, mic and speaker as OK; local preview renders; mic meter moves on speech; Proceed button enabled

Title: Device check blocks join when camera permission is denied
Preconditions: Patient on join page; browser camera permission set to Block
Test_Steps: 1. Open the join link | 2. Start the device self-test | 3. Deny or have camera permission blocked | 4. Attempt to proceed to the session
Expected_Result: Wizard surfaces a clear remediation message naming the blocked permission; Proceed is disabled; link to browser settings shown; no session is created

Title: Bandwidth estimation classifies connection quality at boundary thresholds
Preconditions: Network-shaping tool available to throttle uplink/downlink
Test_Steps: 1. Throttle connection to just below the minimum supported bitrate | 2. Run the connectivity check | 3. Raise to exactly the minimum threshold | 4. Re-run the check
Expected_Result: Below threshold shows Poor with warning to switch to phone; at and above threshold shows Acceptable/Good; classification matches documented thresholds

Title: Connectivity check recommends audio-only fallback under high packet loss
Preconditions: Connectivity check supports loss/jitter simulation
Test_Steps: 1. Set packet loss and jitter combinations | 2. Run check for each combination | 3. Record the recommended mode
Expected_Result: Each loss/jitter pairing yields the documented recommendation per the decision table; recommendation text is unambiguous

Title: Patient enters virtual waiting room before provider admits
Preconditions: Patient passed device/connectivity checks; provider not yet joined
Test_Steps: 1. Complete pre-visit checks | 2. Tap Join | 3. Observe waiting-room state | 4. Wait for provider admit action
Expected_Result: Patient is placed in Waiting state with reassuring status text and estimated wait; patient remains in waiting until provider admits; no media flows to provider yet

Title: Waiting-room queue ordering reflects appointment time and arrival
Preconditions: Multiple patients waiting for the same provider
Test_Steps: 1. Have three patients join in mixed order relative to appointment times | 2. Open the provider queue view | 3. Inspect ordering and badges
Test_Data: P1 appt=10:00 arrived 10:05; P2 appt=10:15 arrived 09:55; P3 appt=10:00 arrived 09:50
Expected_Result: Queue orders deterministically per documented rule (appointment time then arrival); late/early badges accurate; counts correct

Title: Provider sees real-time queue updates when a patient leaves the waiting room
Preconditions: Two patients in the provider waiting queue
Test_Steps: 1. Provider opens queue | 2. One patient closes their tab / disconnects | 3. Observe queue after disconnect grace period
Expected_Result: After grace period the departed patient transitions to Left/Disconnected and is removed or flagged; remaining patient ordering preserved; no stale entry

Title: Provider admits patient and bidirectional video/audio establishes
Preconditions: Patient in waiting room; provider logged in with media permissions
Test_Steps: 1. Provider clicks Admit on the waiting patient | 2. Both grant media | 3. Observe negotiation | 4. Confirm two-way video and audio
Expected_Result: ICE/peer negotiation completes; both parties see remote video and hear audio within acceptable setup time; connection indicator shows Connected

Title: Session falls back to TURN relay when direct peer connection is blocked
Preconditions: Symmetric NAT or firewall blocking direct UDP; TURN server configured
Test_Steps: 1. Force network conditions blocking host/srflx candidates | 2. Start session | 3. Inspect ICE candidate selection | 4. Verify media flows
Expected_Result: Connection negotiates via relay (TURN) candidate; media still establishes; quality indicator may note relayed path; no session failure

Title: Session start fails gracefully when no TURN/STUN reachable
Preconditions: All ICE servers unreachable
Test_Steps: 1. Block STUN/TURN endpoints | 2. Attempt to start session | 3. Wait for negotiation timeout
Expected_Result: Negotiation times out with a clear error; user is offered retry and phone-fallback options; failure is logged for audit; no indefinite spinner

Title: Adaptive bitrate downgrades video resolution under degrading bandwidth
Preconditions: Active session with network shaping available
Test_Steps: 1. Establish session at good bandwidth | 2. Gradually reduce uplink bandwidth | 3. Observe resolution/framerate adaptation | 4. Restore bandwidth
Expected_Result: Encoder steps down resolution/framerate to maintain call; quality indicator updates; on restore it steps back up; no disconnect

Title: Mute/unmute audio and disable/enable video reflect on both ends
Preconditions: Active two-party session
Test_Steps: 1. Provider mutes mic | 2. Patient observes muted indicator | 3. Provider disables camera | 4. Provider re-enables both
Expected_Result: Mute and camera-off states propagate within seconds; remote shows correct indicators/placeholder avatar; re-enabling restores streams

Title: Mid-call device switch (swap microphone) keeps session alive
Preconditions: Active session; two audio input devices available
Test_Steps: 1. During call open device settings | 2. Switch microphone to second device | 3. Speak and confirm audio | 4. Switch back
Expected_Result: Audio track replaces seamlessly without renegotiating the whole session; remote hears new device; no drop

Title: Provider shares screen and patient views shared content
Preconditions: Active session; provider on a browser supporting screen capture
Test_Steps: 1. Provider clicks Share screen | 2. Select a window/tab/screen | 3. Patient confirms visibility | 4. Provider stops sharing
Expected_Result: Patient sees shared content clearly; a sharing indicator is visible to both; stopping returns to camera view cleanly

Title: Screen share auto-stops and warns when sharing a window with PHI to wrong audience
Preconditions: Active session; provider attempts to share a window containing another patient's chart
Test_Steps: 1. Provider initiates share of a chart window | 2. System detects/prompts PHI exposure risk | 3. Provider confirms or cancels
Expected_Result: User is warned before PHI is shared to the patient; accidental cross-patient disclosure is prevented or requires explicit confirmation; action audited

Title: Only one participant can share at a time (concurrency control)
Preconditions: Both participants in session
Test_Steps: 1. Provider starts screen share | 2. Patient attempts to start screen share simultaneously
Expected_Result: Second share request is blocked or the first is cleanly preempted per documented policy; no garbled dual-share; indicators stay consistent

Title: Provider documents an encounter note during the live session and it links to the visit
Preconditions: Active telemedicine visit tied to an OpenMRS visit/encounter on the patient dashboard
Test_Steps: 1. Open the clinical form panel during the call | 2. Enter encounter note text | 3. Save the note | 4. Verify it appears under the active visit
Expected_Result: Encounter saves with correct provider, encounter datetime and visit linkage; note appears under Recent encounters; telehealth encounter type recorded

Title: Encounter created during telehealth visit records encounter type and location as virtual
Preconditions: Telehealth encounter saved; FHIR R4 endpoint accessible
Test_Steps: 1. Save a telehealth encounter | 2. GET the Encounter resource via FHIR R4 | 3. Inspect class, type and location
Expected_Result: Encounter.class indicates virtual (e.g. VR); telehealth type coding present; serviceType/location reflect virtual care; resource validates against R4

Title: Provider places a lab order during the live session
Preconditions: Active telehealth visit; provider with ordering privileges; orderable lab configured
Test_Steps: 1. Open order entry during the session | 2. Search and select a lab test | 3. Complete required order fields | 4. Sign/save the order
Expected_Result: Lab order is created and linked to the telehealth encounter/visit; order status Active; appears in patient orders with correct provider and datetime

Title: Provider e-prescribes with interaction and allergy checks firing
Preconditions: Active telehealth visit; patient has a recorded allergy and an active interacting medication
Test_Steps: 1. Open e-prescribe | 2. Select a drug that interacts with current med and matches allergy | 3. Review alerts | 4. Override with reason or cancel
Test_Data: Patient allergy=Penicillin; New Rx=Amoxicillin; Existing med=Warfarin + interacting drug
Expected_Result: Allergy alert and drug-drug interaction alert both fire; prescribing blocked or requires documented override reason; override reason captured and audited

Title: Controlled-substance e-prescription requires step-up authentication (EPCS)
Preconditions: Provider attempts to prescribe a controlled substance via telehealth
Test_Steps: 1. Open e-prescribe | 2. Select a Schedule II controlled substance | 3. Sign the prescription | 4. Complete step-up auth challenge
Expected_Result: System enforces two-factor/step-up authentication before transmission; without successful challenge the Rx is not sent; event audited per EPCS requirements

Title: E-prescription transmits as a valid FHIR MedicationRequest
Preconditions: Telehealth e-prescription saved; FHIR R4 endpoint accessible
Test_Steps: 1. E-prescribe a medication | 2. GET MedicationRequest for the patient | 3. Validate fields
Expected_Result: MedicationRequest references the patient, requester (provider), encounter and medication; status active; dosageInstruction populated; validates against R4

Title: Recording cannot start until explicit patient consent is captured
Preconditions: Active session; recording feature enabled for the org
Test_Steps: 1. Provider clicks Start recording | 2. Consent prompt shown to patient | 3. Patient grants consent | 4. Recording begins
Expected_Result: Recording does not begin until consent is recorded; a recording indicator shows for both parties once started; consent timestamp/identity stored

Title: Patient declines recording and session continues unrecorded
Preconditions: Provider initiated recording request
Test_Steps: 1. Provider requests recording | 2. Patient declines consent | 3. Observe session and recording state
Expected_Result: No recording is created; provider is notified of decline; session continues normally; decline is logged in the audit trail

Title: Two-party-consent jurisdiction enforces consent from both participants
Preconditions: Org configured for jurisdiction-based consent rules
Test_Steps: 1. Set jurisdiction requiring all-party consent | 2. One party consents, other does not | 3. Both consent | 4. Observe recording permission
Expected_Result: Recording allowed only when all required parties consent per jurisdiction; single-party consent insufficient in two-party jurisdiction; decisions audited

Title: Stored recording is encrypted and access is restricted and audited
Preconditions: A recording exists; user without rights and user with rights available
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to access recording as unauthorized user | 2. Access as authorized user | 3. Inspect storage encryption and audit log
Expected_Result: Unauthorized access denied; authorized access logged with who/when/why; recording stored encrypted at rest; URL not publicly guessable

Title: Provider ends session and is prompted to complete documentation
Preconditions: Active session with note not yet signed
Test_Steps: 1. Provider clicks End session | 2. Observe end-of-visit prompt | 3. Attempt to leave without signing | 4. Complete and sign documentation
Expected_Result: On end, provider is prompted to complete/sign documentation; leaving with unsigned note warns and retains draft; signing transitions encounter to completed

Title: Ending the session closes media for both parties and updates visit status
Preconditions: Active two-party session linked to an OpenMRS visit
Test_Steps: 1. Provider ends the session | 2. Patient client observes disconnect | 3. Check visit/encounter status
Expected_Result: Both media streams torn down; patient sees a session-ended screen; visit/encounter status updated appropriately; no orphaned media connection

Title: Visit duration and start/end times are captured accurately for billing
Preconditions: Completed telehealth session
Test_Steps: 1. Note real session start and end times | 2. End the session | 3. Query stored visit/encounter timestamps
Expected_Result: Stored session start and end timestamps match observed times within tolerance; duration derivable; timezone consistent; values feed billing correctly

Title: Provider switches to phone fallback when video fails mid-visit
Preconditions: Active video session degrading badly; phone number on file
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger video failure / poor quality | 2. Provider selects Switch to phone | 3. Confirm callback flow | 4. Continue visit by phone
Expected_Result: Video session transitions to phone-fallback state; callback initiated to patient number; visit continues and remains the same encounter; switch logged

Title: Phone fallback masks provider's personal number
Preconditions: Phone fallback configured with number masking/proxy
Test_Steps: 1. Initiate phone fallback | 2. Patient receives call | 3. Inspect caller ID presented
Expected_Result: Patient sees the clinic/proxy number, not the provider's personal line; reverse direction also masked; mapping stored securely

Title: Audio-only telehealth visit still produces a complete documented encounter
Preconditions: Visit conducted entirely by phone fallback
Test_Steps: 1. Conduct an audio-only visit | 2. Document the encounter | 3. Verify modality recorded
Expected_Result: Encounter records modality as audio-only/telephone; documentation complete; billing modality flag set correctly for audio-only telehealth

Title: Patient joins from mobile browser and core controls are usable
Preconditions: Patient with mobile device and supported mobile browser
Test_Steps: 1. Open join link on mobile | 2. Grant permissions | 3. Join session | 4. Use mute, camera flip and end controls
Expected_Result: Session works on mobile; front/rear camera flip works; controls are reachable and tappable; layout responsive; no critical control offscreen

Title: Browser/OS pairwise matrix for join compatibility
Preconditions: Test lab with multiple browser/OS/network combinations
Test_Steps: 1. For each pairwise combination join a session | 2. Verify media establishes | 3. Record outcome
Test_Data: Pairs: Chrome/Win/WiFi, Safari/iOS/4G, Firefox/Mac/WiFi, Edge/Win/Ethernet, ChromeAndroid/4G
Expected_Result: Each pairwise combination establishes media and core controls function; any unsupported combo shows a clear unsupported-browser message pre-join

Title: Session continuity when patient switches network from WiFi to cellular
Preconditions: Active session on a mobile device with both WiFi and cellular
Test_Steps: 1. Start session on WiFi | 2. Disable WiFi forcing cellular handover | 3. Observe reconnection
Expected_Result: Session attempts ICE restart/reconnect and recovers within the documented window, or cleanly prompts rejoin; no silent indefinite freeze; encounter preserved

Title: Video controls are keyboard operable and screen-reader labeled
Preconditions: Active session; screen reader available
Test_Steps: 1. Tab through all in-call controls | 2. Activate mute/camera/end via keyboard | 3. Verify screen reader announcements
Expected_Result: All controls reachable and operable by keyboard with visible focus; each has an accessible name/role/state; state changes announced; meets WCAG 2.1 AA

Title: Live captions available for audio during the session
Preconditions: Session with captioning enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Enable captions | 2. Speak during the call | 3. Observe caption rendering and accuracy/latency
Expected_Result: Captions render with reasonable latency and accuracy; can be toggled; do not obscure critical UI; available to hard-of-hearing patients

Title: Join link is single-use/time-bound and rejects reuse after session
Preconditions: A completed or expired telehealth visit with its join link
Test_Steps: 1. Complete a session | 2. Attempt to reopen the same join link | 3. Attempt to use an expired link
Expected_Result: Reusing a consumed or expired link is rejected with a safe message; no unauthorized re-entry into the prior session; attempt is audited

Title: Unauthenticated/wrong patient cannot enter another patient's session
Preconditions: Two patients with separate visits and links
Test_Steps: 1. Patient A obtains a link | 2. Attempt to join Patient B's session with A's identity/link | 3. Tamper visit/room id in URL
Expected_Result: Cross-session entry denied; identity bound to the visit; tampering rejected; no PHI of the other patient exposed; event audited

Title: Media streams are transported over encrypted channels (DTLS-SRTP)
Preconditions: Active session; ability to inspect connection stats
Test_Steps: 1. Start session | 2. Inspect WebRTC connection/transport stats | 3. Verify cipher and DTLS-SRTP usage
Expected_Result: Media is encrypted end-to-end on the transport via DTLS-SRTP; no plaintext RTP; signaling over TLS; no downgrade to unencrypted media

Title: Idle session times out and requires re-auth to resume
Preconditions: Active session left idle near the timeout threshold
Test_Steps: 1. Leave session idle to just before timeout | 2. Confirm still active | 3. Exceed the timeout by one minute | 4. Attempt to resume
Test_Data: Idle timeout=configured; test at T-1, T, T+1
Expected_Result: At or before threshold session remains; beyond threshold session is locked/ended and resuming requires re-authentication; behavior matches configured value

Title: Telehealth lifecycle events are written to the audit log
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; a full telehealth visit performed
Test_Steps: 1. Perform join, admit, consent, recording, order, e-prescribe, end | 2. Open the audit log | 3. Verify each event
Expected_Result: Each lifecycle event is logged with actor, patient, timestamp and action; logs are immutable/tamper-evident; sufficient for HIPAA accountability

Title: Telehealth encounter generates an HL7 v2 ADT message with correct patient class
Preconditions: HL7 v2 interface engine configured; telehealth encounter created
Test_Steps: 1. Create a telehealth encounter | 2. Capture the outbound ADT message | 3. Inspect PV1 segment
Expected_Result: ADT message generated; PV1 patient class and point-of-care reflect virtual/telehealth; MSH well-formed; downstream systems can parse it

Title: Telehealth session API rejects requests missing authorization token
Preconditions: Session management API exposed; valid and missing tokens available
Test_Steps: 1. Call create/join session API without a token | 2. Call with an invalid token | 3. Call with a valid token
Expected_Result: Missing or invalid token returns 401/403 with no PHI; valid token succeeds; error responses do not leak session internals or stack traces

Title: Exploratory: rapid mute/share/end toggling and tab backgrounding stress
Preconditions: Active session; tester free to improvise
Test_Steps: 1. Rapidly toggle mute, camera, screen share | 2. Background and foreground the tab repeatedly | 3. Rotate device; lock/unlock screen | 4. Observe for desync or crashes
Expected_Result: No crash, frozen control, or audio/video desync that cannot recover; indicators remain truthful; session recovers or ends cleanly; defects logged

Title: Order placed in telehealth session honors RBAC for non-prescribing role
Preconditions: User in a non-prescribing role (e.g., MA) in an active session
Test_Steps: 1. Non-prescriber opens order/e-prescribe panel | 2. Attempt to place an order or prescription | 3. Observe authorization outcome
Expected_Result: Action is blocked or routed to provider co-sign per policy; unauthorized order is not created; clear message shown; attempt audited

Title: Provider broadcasts a delay message to waiting patients
Preconditions: One or more patients in the waiting room
Test_Steps: 1. Provider selects a waiting patient or queue | 2. Send a predefined delay message | 3. Patient observes message
Expected_Result: Patient receives the delay/status message in the waiting room; message non-PHI; delivery is timely; does not eject patient from queue

Title: Recording indicator persistence verified across reconnect
Preconditions: Recording active; patient experiences a brief disconnect/reconnect
Test_Steps: 1. Start recording with consent | 2. Force patient brief disconnect | 3. Patient reconnects | 4. Verify indicator and recording state
Expected_Result: After reconnect the recording indicator is restored and accurate; recording state is consistent (continued or restarted per policy); no silent unindicated recording
